commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens `/phone arm` and `/phone disarm` authorization, adds a trusted owner-status exposure path for bundled plugin commands, updates related tests, and adjusts provider registry test mocks.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +158. Total +193 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not a live run: current main only blocks `/phone arm` and `/phone disarm` when gateway scopes are present or the channel is `webchat`, so an external Telegram-style call without gateway scope context can reach the mutation path.
commands.review_followup: The next action is human PR handling because contributor real behavior proof and maintainer approval are required, even though the provider test mock regression is mechanically fixable.

target.title: Tighten phone-control mutation authorization [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Require `operator.admin` scope metadata on the bundled `/phone` plugin command so scoped command dispatch exposes and enforces the intended operator boundary.
- Add a defensive handler check before `/phone arm` and `/phone disarm` mutate `gateway.nodes.allowCommands` or `gateway.nodes.denyCommands`.
- Reverse the external-channel regression expectations so non-owner callers without `operator.admin` are blocked and do not write config.
- Keep owner and explicit `operator.admin` paths covered as allowed behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

N/A

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related private maintainer report.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested through maintainer tracking context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: External channel `/phone arm` and `/phone disarm` callers must be owner/operator-authorized before changing Gateway node command policy.
- Real environment tested: Not tested in a live OpenClaw setup for this branch preparation.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: None; repository command execution was intentionally left for maintainer/CI validation.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Updated regression coverage in `extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts` documents the expected blocked and allowed paths.
- Observed result after fix: Non-owner external callers without `operator.admin` receive an authorization response and config mutation is not called.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram/Discord/Slack command dispatch and end-to-end Gateway config persistence.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This metadata draft did not run repository commands.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing tests previously allowed external channel callers without `operator.admin` to mutate phone-control state.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

None.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts` now checks that external non-owner callers without `operator.admin` cannot arm or disarm phone control, and that owner or `operator.admin` callers remain allowed.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The test contract allowed an external channel caller without `operator.admin` to run `/phone arm` and write Gateway node command policy.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression coverage was updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No schema, environment, or migration behavior changed. The existing config mutation is now gated more narrowly.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, authorization behavior changed for phone-control mutations.

What is the highest-risk area?

Shared-channel users who previously could run `/phone arm` or `/phone disarm` with only generic command authorization will now need owner status or `operator.admin`.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is scoped to the mutation subcommands and preserves explicit owner/admin paths in tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run focused tests for `extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts` and review the scoped authorization behavior.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer/CI validation and optional live channel proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None at draft time.

AI-assisted: Yes
target.author: pgondhi987
